pest_risk: Very High (aphids / thrips / spider mites / rose chafer beetles / whiteflies / scale)
disease_prevention: Ensure good air circulation; avoid overhead watering; water at base only; remove fallen leaves promptly; use disease-resistant varieties; regular preventive fungicide spray; mulch to prevent soil splash onto leaves
disease_treatment: Remove and destroy affected leaves (do not compost); apply copper or sulfur fungicide for black spot/rust; apply potassium bicarbonate or sulfur for powdery mildew; neem oil or insecticidal soap for pests

pest_risk: High (whiteflies / aphids / spider mites / mealybugs / thrips)
disease_prevention: Ensure good air circulation; avoid overhead watering; water at base; maintain adequate nutrition; control pest vectors that spread viral diseases
disease_treatment: Remove affected leaves; apply copper fungicide for leaf spot; sulfur or potassium bicarbonate for powdery mildew; neem oil or insecticidal soap for pests; no cure for viral infection — remove and destroy affected plants

disease_prevention: Ensure good air circulation; avoid overhead watering; avoid waterlogging; prune regularly to maintain open plant structure; control pest vectors
disease_treatment: Remove affected leaves; apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate for powdery mildew; copper fungicide for leaf blight; neem oil for pests; ensure well-draining soil if root rot suspected

pest_risk: Very High (spider mites are a major pest especially in low humidity / also mealybugs / scale / thrips)
disease_prevention: Maintain high humidity to deter spider mites; ensure well-draining soil; avoid overwatering; ensure good air circulation; inspect regularly for early pest detection
disease_treatment: Apply neem oil or insecticidal soap for spider mites and other pests; increase humidity; apply copper fungicide for anthracnose; repot in fresh well-draining mix if root rot detected

pest_risk: Moderate (citrus psyllid / aphids / whiteflies / mealybugs / scale)
disease_prevention: Ensure well-draining soil; avoid overwatering; control citrus psyllid with regular inspection and neem oil; ensure good air circulation; avoid waterlogging
disease_treatment: Apply copper fungicide for leaf spot; neem oil for pests especially citrus psyllid; repot in fresh well-draining mix if root rot detected; remove and destroy heavily affected branches

pest_risk: Low to Moderate (aphids / spider mites / whiteflies — generally resistant due to aromatic oils)
disease_prevention: Ensure good air circulation; avoid overhead watering; pinch flower heads regularly to keep plant bushy and reduce mildew risk; avoid waterlogging; plant in full sun
disease_treatment: Apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate for powdery mildew; copper fungicide for leaf spot; neem oil for pests; ensure well-draining soil if root rot suspected

pest_risk: Low to Moderate (mealybugs in leaf crevices / scale / aloe mites)
disease_prevention: Use well-draining gritty soil; avoid overwatering; ensure pot has drainage holes; allow soil to dry completely between waterings; avoid water sitting in leaf rosette center
disease_treatment: Remove affected roots and leaves; repot in fresh gritty well-draining mix; allow to dry out before rewatering if rot detected; apply neem oil for mealybugs and scale; no recovery possible once severe root rot has set in
